_comment: Single source of truth for AI surfaces and the transitions between them (P43). Consumed by tools/wizard.py (/chatgpt, /transitions, /cross-modality) and mirrored by docs/TRANSITIONS.md. Drift invariant 32 keeps the JSON, the doc, and the wizard surface keys consistent. Every OpenAI or Google product claim this repo cannot verify from cited research carries a literal [NEEDS VERIFICATION: ...] tag; treat those as things to check against your own plan, never as facts.
_residual_origin_note: origin 'other' is the forward-compatibility residual in tools/handoff/queue.py ALLOWED_ORIGINS; it names no surface by design and is claimed here so invariant 55's origin-coverage check is total.

claude_desktop.label: Claude Desktop (this computer)
setup_steps: Install Claude Desktop and keep your Creator OS folder on this computer.
Run the wizard (python3 tools/wizard.py) and pick Claude Desktop; it writes the MCP entries to your Claude Desktop config.
Restart Claude Desktop; the Creator OS tools appear automatically.

claude_code.label: Claude Code / command line
setup_steps: Open a terminal in your Creator OS folder.
Run tools directly (python3 tools/...), or use the same MCP server as Claude Desktop.

claude_web.label: claude.ai in a browser (web and mobile)
setup_steps: For knowledge-only use, add the Creator OS knowledge files to a claude.ai Project.

For live tools, you or your developer must deploy the remote MCP endpoint behind HTTPS with authentication (implementation/gpt/mcp-connector/README.md), then add it in claude.ai as a custom connector.

cowork_local.label: Claude Cowork (local session on this computer)
setup_steps: Install Creator OS through the plugin marketplace.
Open the Desktop app's Cowork tab and choose a local session (a hypervisor-isolated VM on this computer).
Connect the Creator OS folder so the session can reach your files.
needs_verification: STT backend presence inside the local VM (transcription is native only if a backend is installed)
connected-folder reliability for large media files

cowork_remote.label: Claude Cowork (remote ephemeral sandbox)
setup_steps: Install Creator OS through the plugin marketplace; an organization's plugin auto-sync fires only when a version-bump PR merges.
Reach stateful data through Google Drive or a hosted connector; the sandbox is destroyed at session end, so nothing written to its local disk (including .local store files) persists.
Local stdio MCP servers do not run here; connect capabilities as remote MCP connectors, and reach local files only through folders explicitly connected in the Desktop app.
needs_verification: whether a repo clone behaves identically to Claude Code on the web

chatgpt_web_plain.label: ChatGPT web chat (plain chat at chatgpt.com)
setup_steps: Open ChatGPT Settings, then Personalization, then Custom Instructions.
Paste the two boxes from implementation/gpt/web/custom-instructions.md (the file tells you which text goes in which box).
This is knowledge-only mode: no live data, no local tools, no capability flags. Numbers you did not supply in the chat are never real Creator OS data.
At the start of a session, upload your latest dated export file if you keep one (see the read-back steps in docs/TRANSITIONS.md).

chatgpt_custom_gpt.label: Custom GPT (built in the ChatGPT GPT builder)
setup_steps: Ask Creator OS (on your computer) for a Custom GPT export; the export-gpt package gives you an instruction file and up to 20 knowledge files.
In the GPT builder, paste the instruction file and upload the knowledge files.
Optionally add the jurisdiction Action: paste implementation/gpt/actions/jurisdiction_overlay_action.yaml as an Action with authentication set to none.
Privacy note: an Action sends what you type (for example an address) to OpenAI and to the public endpoint it calls; the local ask-first consent step does not apply here (see docs/PASTE-SAFETY.md).
needs_verification: knowledge file count and size limits in the GPT builder for your plan

chatgpt_projects.label: ChatGPT Projects (a Project with files at chatgpt.com)
setup_steps: Create a Project in ChatGPT and set the Project instructions to the instruction file from a Custom GPT export (same pack works here).
Upload the knowledge files to the Project files area. [NEEDS VERIFICATION: Project file count and size limits for your plan]
Knowledge-only mode applies: no live tools, no flags. Keep the packaging version line so you can tell when to re-upload.
needs_verification: Project file count and size limits
whether Project instructions have a length limit on your plan

chatgpt_desktop.label: ChatGPT desktop app
setup_steps: Install the ChatGPT desktop app and sign in.
For knowledge-only use, paste implementation/gpt/web/custom-instructions.md into your custom instructions, exactly like the web version.
For live Creator OS tools: open Settings, then Connectors, and enable developer mode if your plan offers it. [NEEDS VERIFICATION: developer-mode connector availability depends on your ChatGPT plan]
Add your deployed Creator OS remote MCP endpoint as a connector (implementation/gpt/mcp-connector/README.md). Without a deployed endpoint there are no live tools here.
Capability flags are not enforced on this surface; the connector endpoint enforces its own gates on the machine where it runs.
needs_verification: developer-mode MCP connector availability and plan gating

gemini_api.label: Gemini API (developer integration)
setup_steps: Load implementation/gemini/*function-declarations.json as functionDeclarations in your app.
When Gemini returns a function call, YOUR app executes the HTTPS request and returns the result.
Flags are enforced only if your backend runs the Creator OS Python tools.

gemini_gems.label: Gemini Gems (consumer)
setup_steps: Paste implementation/gemini/system-instruction.md into a Gem.
Gems cannot call custom tools; this is knowledge-only. For live data, move to the Gemini API or a Claude surface.

needs_export: the knowledge pack (custom-instructions.md, pasted into ChatGPT settings)
your freshness or task data as a dated export file (export-and-you-save)
stops_working: all Class C tools: finance math, document-template assembly, obligation date math, the deterministic quality score
capability flag enforcement (nothing evaluates your local config there)
all local stores: rate card, deals, contracts, templates, profile
live connectors and publishing gates
reimport_steps: paste the two custom-instruction boxes once
at the start of each ChatGPT session, upload your latest dated export file if you want it considered
flags_unenforced: ALL capability flags: contract, finance, publishing, templates
notes: Read docs/PASTE-SAFETY.md before pasting anything: rate card numbers, contract text, and personal identity details do not belong in a third-party chat without a deliberate decision.

needs_export: a Custom GPT package from export-gpt (instruction file + up to 20 knowledge files)
stops_working: all Class C tools
capability flag enforcement
local stores
the local ask-first consent step for live lookups (the Action calls public endpoints from OpenAI's side)
reimport_steps: paste the instruction file in the GPT builder
upload the knowledge files
re-export and re-upload when the packaging version changes
notes: The Action privacy note applies: what you type into the GPT goes to OpenAI and to the public endpoint.

needs_export: the same Custom GPT package (instruction + knowledge files) reused as Project instructions + Project files
stops_working: all Class C tools
capability flag enforcement
local stores
reimport_steps: set Project instructions to the instruction file
upload knowledge files to the Project
re-upload when the packaging version changes
notes: Project file and instruction limits vary by plan [NEEDS VERIFICATION: check your plan].

needs_export: the knowledge pack (paste path), OR nothing if you deploy the remote MCP connector
stops_working: without a deployed connector: everything the plain-web transition loses
with a deployed connector: nothing tool-wise, but flag enforcement happens on the machine running the endpoint, not inside ChatGPT
reimport_steps: paste custom instructions for the knowledge baseline
or deploy the remote MCP endpoint (implementation/gpt/mcp-connector/README.md) and add it as a developer-mode connector [NEEDS VERIFICATION: plan gating]
flags_unenforced: flags are evaluated by the deployed endpoint's machine, never by ChatGPT itself
notes: This is the strongest ChatGPT surface for Creator OS because of the connector option.

needs_export: knowledge files into a claude.ai Project, OR deploy the remote MCP connector for live tools
stops_working: without a connector: Class C tools, flags, local stores
with a connector: nothing tool-wise; flags enforce on the endpoint's machine
reimport_steps: upload the Project knowledge files
or add the deployed endpoint as a claude.ai custom connector

needs_export: the ChatGPT knowledge pack (claude.ai Project files do not transfer; re-paste the ChatGPT custom instructions)
stops_working: any remote MCP connector you used on claude.ai (plain ChatGPT web cannot use it)
reimport_steps: paste custom-instructions.md into ChatGPT settings
carry data across as dated export files you upload per session
flags_unenforced: ALL capability flags on both surfaces
notes: Neither surface can merge data files; the newest dated file wins. Your computer's tools merge properly when the files come home.

needs_export: your ChatGPT profile answers via the profile-import prompt (implementation/gpt/profile-import/PROMPT.md)
any dated export files you accumulated
reimport_steps: run the profile-import prompt once per ChatGPT context (default chat, each Project, each custom GPT) and paste the JSON back home
the profile-import atom proposes creator-profile.local.json with per-field provenance; you review and save it by hand
drop dated export files into your store folder; the local tools union-merge them automatically
notes: Everything gains enforcement again on this transition: flags, consent gates, deterministic tools.

travels_automatically: your ChatGPT account context (memory) may carry over [NEEDS VERIFICATION: memory behavior inside GPTs varies]
needs_export: the Custom GPT package (instruction + knowledge files); custom instructions do not apply inside a GPT
reimport_steps: build the GPT from the export-gpt package
re-run the profile-import prompt inside the GPT context if you rely on profile answers there
flags_unenforced: ALL capability flags on both surfaces

needs_export: reuse the same instruction + knowledge files as Project instructions + files
stops_working: Actions (Projects have no Actions; the jurisdiction lookup stops working)
reimport_steps: create the Project, paste instructions, upload files
flags_unenforced: ALL capability flags on both surfaces

travels_automatically: your ChatGPT account, chats, memory, and custom instructions (same account)
reimport_steps: nothing for the knowledge baseline
optionally add a deployed remote MCP connector in developer mode for live tools [NEEDS VERIFICATION: plan gating]
flags_unenforced: ALL capability flags unless a deployed connector enforces them on its own machine
notes: This is the easiest upgrade: same account, plus the connector option.

needs_export: profile answers via the profile-import prompt
reimport_steps: same as the plain-web return: profile-import proposal, hand-save, drop dated files into the store

needs_export: the Gem instruction pack (export-gem; 10-file knowledge limit)
stops_working: all Class B and C capabilities (Gems cannot call custom tools)
flags
local stores
reimport_steps: paste the Gem instruction
re-paste when the packaging version changes
notes: Gems are the most limited surface; only pure-reasoning skills work.

needs_export: the Custom GPT package (the Gem pack is Gemini-shaped; export fresh for the GPT builder)
reimport_steps: build the GPT from the export-gpt package; the jurisdiction Action becomes available again
flags_unenforced: ALL capability flags on both surfaces

travels_automatically: everything: same computer, same files, same tools, same flags
reimport_steps: run the wizard once so Claude Desktop's config carries the MCP entries
